sp_widget.name: tracenow_privacy_policy

Back to Dashboard

Privacy Policy

This document describes how TraceNow handles data collected in the course of providing our ServiceNow instance migration platform. We are committed to transparency, data minimization, and the security of your information.

CONTENTS
  • Introduction
  • Data We Collect
  • How We Use Your Data
  • Data Security
  • Security Incident Response

Introduction

TraceNow is committed to protecting your privacy. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, disclose, and safeguard your information when you use TraceNow, our enterprise data migration tool designed to migrate data between ServiceNow instances.

This policy applies to all users of the TraceNow platform, including administrators, migration engineers, and any personnel granted access to the tool within your organization.

By using TraceNow, you agree to the collection and use of information in accordance with this policy.

Data We Collect

We collect the following categories of information in connection with your use of TraceNow:

Account & Authentication Data

Name, email address, job title, organization name, and authentication credentials used to access the TraceNow platform.

ServiceNow Instance Metadata

Instance URLs, connection credentials (stored encrypted), API endpoint configurations, and instance version information.

Usage & Telemetry Data

Feature usage patterns, session duration, error events, and performance metrics collected to improve platform reliability and support diagnostics.

How We Use Your Data

Service Delivery

To authenticate users, execute migration jobs between ServiceNow instances, and deliver the core functionality of TraceNow.

Platform Improvement

To analyze usage patterns, diagnose performance issues, and develop new features that improve the migration experience.

Data Security

Encryption in Transit & at Rest

All data transmitted between your environment and TraceNow is encrypted using TLS 1.2 or higher. Credentials and sensitive configuration values are encrypted at rest using AES-256.

Access Controls

Role-based access controls (RBAC) restrict platform access to authorized personnel within your organization. Administrative functions require multi-factor authentication (MFA).

Security Incident Response

In the event of a security incident affecting your data, TraceNow will:

  1. Contain and investigate the incident promptly upon discovery.
  2. Notify affected customers within 72 hours of confirming a breach that poses a risk to personal data.
  3. Provide a detailed incident report including the nature of the breach, data categories affected, likely consequences, and remediation steps taken.

To report a security concern, contact: security@tracenow.io

Contact Us

If you have questions, concerns, or requests related to this Privacy Policy or the handling of your data, please contact our Privacy team:

General Privacy
privacy@tracenow.io
Security Incidents
security@tracenow.io
Policy version 2.0 · Effective June 9, 2026
Back to top
